canonical_definition: A complete submission to the CAA containing all regulatory evidence, safety cases, operational procedures, and technical documentation required for a BVLOS UAS trial to proceed.
notes: Dorothy-8 identified ambiguity: complete has different meanings in application intake (REQ001) vs incident investigation (REQ012).

canonical_definition: Specific Operational Risk Assessment. A regulatory framework (JARUS SORA v2.5) for assessing UAS operational risks.
notes: Acronym not expanded in user story.

canonical_definition: Specific Assurance and Integrity Level. A risk classification level output by SORA assessment that quantifies the rigor of evidence required.
notes: Distinct from OSO. Higher SAIL = stricter requirements.

canonical_definition: Operational Safety Objectives. Specific, measurable safety goals derived from SORA assessment that define required mitigation levels.
notes: Not to be confused with generic requirements.

canonical_definition: Methodology or evidence that explicitly accounts for collective behavior, coordination dependencies, and emergent risks of multiple coordinated aircraft.
notes: Critical undefined term. Requires workshop to validate definition.

canonical_definition: Active, informed crew awareness and retained authority to perceive system state, understand autonomous actions, and intervene before safety-critical decisions.
notes: Requires measurable criteria: situational awareness frequency, authority preservation, takeover time, workload limits.

canonical_definition: In a manner that can be justified through evidence and logical argument to regulatory scrutiny, peer review, and accident investigation.
notes: No criteria given. Requires reference to JARUS methodology and CAA precedent.

canonical_definition: Systems combining mechanical, software, and procedural measures that prevent UAS from exiting operational boundary regardless of link loss or autonomy failure.
notes: Five critical subcriteria undefined: boundary tolerance, fail-safe mechanism, design standard, environmental factors, verification approach.

canonical_definition: Virtual 3D boundary (lat/lon/altitude/time) defining the trial operational area. Geofence violations trigger containment actions.
notes: Distinct from containment (which is the mechanism; geofence is the boundary).

canonical_definition: Loss of control of UAS such that it exits authorized airspace and cannot be recovered by operator.
notes: Domain jargon defining the failure mode that containment prevents.

canonical_definition: Command and Control link - communication pathway between ground crew and UAS for transmitting commands and receiving status/telemetry.
notes: Acronym must be expanded on first use.

canonical_definition: Communication pathways between UAS aircraft within swarm for coordination, de-confliction, or status exchange.
notes: Distinct from C2 (ground-to-air). Assumes swarm has aircraft-to-aircraft comms.

canonical_definition: UAS and swarm have procedures and design such that loss of C2 or inter-vehicle comms can be managed without loss of separation or collision.
notes: Ambiguous: time-to-loss undefined, action undefined, crew role unclear. Requires workshop.

canonical_definition: Operational Design Domain - set of operating conditions (environmental, technical, geographical, temporal) for which UAS and autonomy systems have been validated and approved.
notes: Standard in SAE J3161. Critical for autonomy assurance.

canonical_definition: Concept of Operations - narrative description of trial conduct including flight profiles, crew roles, decision points, and normal procedures.
notes: Standard aerospace term.

canonical_definition: Expected, planned system performance under normal operating conditions as described in ConOps.
notes: Paired with worst-credible behavior. Threshold should be defined in ConOps.

canonical_definition: Most severe adverse system performance or failure mode reasonably foreseeable that must be survived by containment, backup systems, or crew intervention.
notes: Boundary between credible and incredible undefined. Requires workshop: single-failure assumption, common-mode criteria, environmental extremes.

canonical_definition: Temporary Danger Area - designated airspace volume and time window reserved for trial, excluding other civilian traffic. Established by NOTAM and managed by ATSU.
notes: CAA-specific. Distinct from TRA (Temporary Reserved Area).

canonical_definition: Notice to Airmen - notice issued by aeronautical authorities to alert operators to potential hazards. TDA trials must be published via NOTAM.
notes: Standard aviation term. Format defined by ICAO Annex 15.

canonical_definition: Air Traffic Service Unit - regulatory organization responsible for monitoring airspace, issuing clearances, and managing NOTAMs and TDAs.
notes: ATSU is approval gate and stakeholder for trials.

canonical_definition: Detect-and-Avoid - capability for UAS to autonomously sense, track, and resolve conflicts with other aircraft without relying on ground crew or external feeds.
notes: DO-365 and F3442 are operative standards. Swarm DAA is more complex than single-aircraft.

canonical_definition: Conflicts with other aircraft are resolved deterministically and foreseeable to all participants. Requires defined avoidance maneuver selection logic, coordination method, crew awareness, and verification.
notes: Requirement states goal but not mechanism. Requires workshop to define preferred maneuvers and verification.

canonical_definition: Design of how collective decisions are made by swarm: centralized (ground-based), distributed (peer voting), or hierarchical (lead decides). Must define: decision authority, communication topology, failure modes, crew override.
notes: REQ008 requires assurance but does not specify architecture. Requires workshop.

canonical_definition: Behavior arising from collective swarm interactions not explicitly programmed in individual aircraft. Can be desired (formation cohesion) or undesired (deadlock, separation loss).
notes: High-risk term. Requires formal methods or Monte Carlo verification.

canonical_definition: System (particularly ML models) whose decisions can be reconstructed and explained post-hoc through investigation of model weights, training data, decision logic, and failure modes.
notes: Perfect post-hoc explainability for deep learning is infeasible. Requires workshop to define acceptable techniques and fallback.

canonical_definition: ML model whose weights, architecture, training data, and hyperparameters are fixed and immutable for trial duration. Enables consistent behavior and forensic investigation.
notes: Seven subcriteria undefined: data retention, duration, security, manifest format, proprietary handling, ownership, reconstruction timing. Requires workshop.

canonical_definition: Assurance Case for Machine Learning in Autonomous Systems - structured argument that ML components meet safety, robustness, and assurance requirements.
acronym_expansion: Assurance Case for Machine Learning in Autonomous Systems
notes: Emerging standard aligned with IEC 62304 and IEC 61508.

canonical_definition: Comprehensive telemetry, video, audio, and state logs recorded throughout flight enabling forensic reconstruction of all system decisions, failures, and incidents.
notes: Different meaning than completeness in REQ001. Requires workshop to define telemetry scope, sampling rate, retention.

canonical_definition: Mandatory Occurrence Report - legal obligation to report accidents, incidents, and safety-relevant anomalies to CAA within defined timeframes.
notes: System must auto-file or provide explicit MOR interface.

canonical_definition: Data Protection Impact Assessment - structured analysis required by GDPR assessing how personal data is collected, processed, and protected.
notes: GDPR Article 35 requirement for high-risk personal data processing.

canonical_definition: Cognitive and physical demands on crew during trial: number of concurrent decisions, decision frequency, time available to respond. Excessive workload compromises meaningful human control.
notes: REQ010 requires evidence but does not define acceptable thresholds. Requires workshop.

canonical_definition: Action by crew to regain full control from autonomous systems during off-nominal event. Must be: evident to crew, rapid (<=X seconds), complete (full authority), reversible.
notes: REQ010 requires evidence but does not specify time, authority, or reversibility. Requires workshop.

canonical_definition: System forcing all swarm aircraft to cease flight immediately, independent of C2 link, inter-vehicle links, or onboard autonomy. Triggered by dedicated ground switch, RF kill-switch, or crew manual intervention.
notes: REQ011 requires it but does not specify mechanism, time-to-effect, or verification. Requires workshop.

canonical_definition: Table mapping off-nominal events (GPS loss, link loss, geofence breach, airspace intrusion, crew incapacitation) to response actions (Return-to-Launch, hold, terminate).
notes: Contingency matrix is the state-machine specification for onboard autonomy. Requires workshop to define format and coverage.

canonical_definition: Authorization permitting trial with explicit restrictions: scope limits (VLOS only, <2 aircraft), conditions (CAA observer, real-time monitoring), gates (no Phase 2 without review).
notes: One of three regulatory decision states. Conditions are enforceable.

canonical_definition: Authorization decision denying trial. Must include explicit reasons and path to re-application if deficiencies are addressed.
notes: One of three decision states. Refusal is reversible.

canonical_definition: Authorization decision requesting additional evidence, analysis, or clarification before final decision. Specifies which requirements are unclear and what evidence would resolve.
notes: One of three decision states. Distinct from outright refusal.

canonical_definition: Phased trial structure where each phase (Phase 1 VLOS, Phase 2 BVLOS, Phase 3 swarm) has entry/exit gates. Reversal to previous phase possible if off-nominal events exceed risk threshold.
notes: REQ016 requires gates but does not specify trigger conditions, process, or state transition. Requires workshop.

canonical_definition: Civil Aviation Authority - UK regulatory body certifying aircraft, approving trial operations, investigating accidents. CAA approval is gate for Operational Authorisation.
notes: Primary regulatory approval authority.

canonical_definition: Military Aviation Authority - UK regulatory body for military aviation. For MOD trials or classified systems, MAA approval required in addition to CAA.
notes: CAA/MAA Joint Approval Workbench coordinates across civil and military.

canonical_definition: Visual Line of Sight - operating aircraft with direct visual contact maintained by pilot at all times. Baseline for manned aviation and baseline trial type.
notes: Opposite of BVLOS. Standard aviation term.

canonical_definition: Beyond Visual Line of Sight - operating aircraft without direct visual contact, relying on instrumentation, cameras, or remote monitoring.
notes: Opposite of VLOS. Standard aviation term.
